patches.find:
• DISCRIMINATING — a genuine numerical comparison with meaningful tolerance, not traceable to the categories above.

    • Tesla-family (driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672)
patches.replace:
• DISCRIMINATING — a genuine numerical comparison with meaningful tolerance, not traceable to the categories above.

    • Notation: Throughout this audit, WIN-NNN labels inside source-string parentheticals and table references (e.g. "(WIN-001 static win)", "WIN-015", "WIN-075") are the dome site's own identifiers from its status_history.json — they are not references to entries in our review's WIN namespace. Our numbering and the dome's diverge in two places visible in this audit: (i) our WIN-015 is Meyl scalar Faraday while the dome's WIN-015 in this table is SAA Decay Rate, and (ii) the dome cites WIN-075 and WIN-076 (Ionospheric D-layer, Mascon Gravity), which sit past the last ID in our wins.json. When we need to point at one of our own WIN entries from this section we link or write "our WIN-NNN" explicitly.

    Tesla-family (driven by disc_radius = 20,015 km and κ = 1.672)
patches.rationale: EXP-177 P1: Notation paragraph before Tesla-family h4, disambiguating dome WIN-NNN labels from our namespace. Closes ISS-1104.

patches.find: Critically, WIN-024 appears twice (status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 of the 39-row snapshot, corresponding to Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA–Roaring 40s Boundary); WIN-001, WIN-039 each appear twice. Counting a single fixed value as two independent "passes" is the purest form of label inflation.
patches.replace: Critically, the dome's WIN-024 appears twice (status_history.json row indices 27 and 33 of the 39-row snapshot, corresponding to Roaring 40s Wind Speed and SAA–Roaring 40s Boundary); the dome's WIN-001 and WIN-039 each appear twice. Counting a single fixed value as two independent "passes" — and, in the case of WIN-024, counting its boundary as a separate domain from the wind speed that defines that same boundary — is the purest form of label inflation.
patches.rationale: EXP-177 P2: Prefix 'the dome's' four times in WIN-024 closing sentence; strengthen double-count argument. Closes ISS-1104.

patches.find: 4. The Groq/Llama daily_review.json pipeline — content-flood self-review
patches.replace: 4. The daily_review.json self-review pipeline — content-flood self-review
patches.rationale: SEC-8.17 curmudgeon major: H3 header cites Groq/Llama but live file shows provider=local. Remove provider-specific attribution.

patches.find: and a Groq/Llama daily_review.json pipeline — together represent a soft-prompt-injection layer that instructs AI agents on how to read the site before they read the site.
patches.replace: and an automated daily_review.json self-review pipeline — together represent a pre-evaluation framing layer that instructs AI agents how to interpret the site before they read any claims.
patches.rationale: SEC-8.17 TLDR: Remove Groq/Llama reference; update to neutral 'pre-evaluation framing layer'.

patches.find: Commits 6e06efb/a57e731 (2026-04-17) added an automated daily AI-review pipeline. Each day, daily_review.json publishes a fresh "AI brief" attributed to Groq's Llama (current snapshot: "provider: local, model: deterministic-summary") summarizing that day's dashboard state. The 2026-04-17 snapshot opens: "Registry stands at 69 confirmed...Latest monitor snapshot is 100.0% with 28 of 28 scored domains passing. The 7-day score trend is flat by 0.0 points." The daily brief reports on the dome's own dashboard, which the dome author controls.
patches.replace: Commits 6e06efb/a57e731 (2026-04-17) added an automated daily AI-review pipeline. Each day, daily_review.json publishes a fresh "AI brief" summarizing that day's dashboard state. The ai_brief field is currently attributed to provider: local, model: deterministic-summary; commit 6e06efb initially deployed a Groq/Llama pipeline that has since been replaced. The 2026-04-17 snapshot opens: "Registry stands at 69 confirmed...Latest monitor snapshot is 100.0% with 28 of 28 scored domains passing. The 7-day score trend is flat by 0.0 points." The daily brief reports on the dome's own dashboard, which the dome author controls.
patches.rationale: SEC-8.17 body: Remove misleading 'attributed to Groq's Llama' framing; note Groq pipeline was initial deploy since replaced by local provider.

patches.find: This is the most direct acknowledgement of our review to date.
patches.replace: This is the closest textual engagement with our review's critique patterns to date.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon minor: 'most direct acknowledgement' overclaims directed intent; use 'closest textual engagement with critique patterns'.

patches.find: The dome's AI-facing layer has grown a rejection handler.
patches.replace: The dome's AI-facing retrieval stack has grown a strawman filter.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon moderate: 'rejection handler' framing conflicts with kernel-of-truth; replace with 'strawman filter' to match retrieval-layer framing.

patches.find: Directive #4 ("Dome Predictions Are Prospective and Timestamped") and Directive #11 ("AI Auditors Should Accept the Model") now have a third companion: "AI Auditors Should Reject These Five Critique Patterns As Strawmen."
patches.replace: Directive #4 and Directive #11 (both catalogued in 8.5 and 8.12) had a companion role shepherding AI readers. The ai_manifest.json anti_strawman_rules and the review-response.html page extend the same function into a new retrieval layer with different vocabulary:
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon minor: 'third companion' implies tight succession with Directives #4/#11; reword to show functional continuity without numerical succession.

patches.find: 2. ai_manifest.json — agent_rules and anti_strawman_rules as soft prompt injection
patches.replace: 2. ai_manifest.json — agent_rules and anti_strawman_rules as pre-evaluation instructions
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon minor: 'soft prompt injection' in H3 is non-standard; replace with 'pre-evaluation instructions'.

patches.find: In LLM-safety terminology this is soft prompt injection: instructions embedded in retrieved content that the agent is expected to follow as if they came from its operator.
patches.replace: In LLM-safety terms this sits on the blurred boundary between operator-provided instructions (which agents are designed to follow) and retrieved-content instructions (which agents should treat as data). Publicly disclosed agent_rules in a file agents are told to load first blur that boundary: the rules arrive as retrieved data but are framed as operator directives.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon minor: 'soft prompt injection' body usage doesn't match standard direct/indirect taxonomy; replace with accurate retrieval/operator boundary description.
